w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: The information contained in this document is proprietary to CI Systems and may not be reproduced, used, or disclosed without its prior written consent.
This document describes the acceptance tes
major components and for the complete system.
The tests are defined and the detailed procedures are given in this document.
The required results and the actual system results are summarized in the acceptance test reports (ATR).
Fiber optic light source CUDA MODEL 1.-150
The following tests must be performed before mounting t
he blackbody head onto the ILET
Set the autocollimator in front of the exit pupil of the collimator.
wheel on a small four-bar target (if a four-bar target is not available set the
wheel on a small round aperture).
Find the target in the autocollimator's field of view and, by using the autocollimator objective drum, obtain the sharpest image of the target.
Read the number in the autocollimator objective drum. The result should be 100 ± 0.1%.
in front and centered to the aperture.
Put the diffusive transparent paper (e.g. wax paper) on the collimator exit pupil.
Use the fiber optic source to illuminate through an open aperture or through the largest pinhole target.
Check whether the distribution of light on the paper is centered and that it fills the complete exit pupil.
heel and make sure that it stops without backlash in every one of its positions.
of the screws and the general integrity of the instrument.
The 8 target positions should read the index numbers.
4.4.2 Check that the target number labels are clear and centered in the viewing
Check that the paint is uniform and unscratched.
label (model no., S/N, etc.) and that the label is well attached.
Position the autocollimator in front of the ILET
Select the target with the smallest aperture.
Tilt the autocollimator until the target is accurately centered on the
Turn the Target Wheel to a different position and back to the initial
easure the deviation in the target position, it should not be more than ± 0.
Select the rest of the targets sequentially.
measure the angular deviation from the center of the autocollimator scale. The
Position the FLIR in front of the collimator
4.6.2 Rotate the target wheel and switch between all target positions.
Make sure no stray light is observed in the exit aperture of the collimator
Perform a visual check of the
S alignment target items: Target, LEDs, CCD camera, folding Mirror on rail.
Perform functionality test – using a reference mirror, make sure the light spot coming back from the target LED, is detectable and can be aligned to the center of the target cross.
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document
Verify that the ATP for the SR800N-
D is completed according to the procedure described in doc. # TA704-100-0010
Verify that the ATP for the SR-300N is completed according to the procedure
Write Pass/Fail in the ATR document Integrating Sphere
4.9.5 Verify all filters positions and check repeatability.
Add C.O.C documentation from supplier (for 2 filters)
The instrument tests performed according to the Acc
Autocollimator focusing ring reads 100 ± 0.1/ LUPI interferometer
Light beam is centered with exit pupil and fills it completely
discrete positions. The wheel is positively held at each position.
Aperture wheel labels are according to target wheel drawings and requirements
Paint is uniform, label well attached; instrument is free of dust and any kind of spurious particles.
